title: uvifera microphylla
intro: Uvifera microphylla, also known as Uva Ursi, is a type of evergreen shrub native to the mountainous regions of North America and Europe, belonging to the family Ericaceae. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anthraquinone glycosides and tannins. These compounds have been found to have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects, making Uva Ursi beneficial for treating urinary tract infections, diarrhea, and other gastrointestinal issues. In herbal preparations, Uva Ursi is often used to make teas, capsules, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues, including kidney stones and bladder problems, and to support the immune system.
